workflow.name: Write daily tech news with OpenAI using RSS feeds and WordPress
workflow.description:
## Who is this for

Tech bloggers, content creators, and site owners who want a fully automated daily news column without writing a single word.

## What this workflow does

- A schedule trigger fires daily at 8 AM and pulls articles from four RSS feeds (BBC Tech, The Verge, Ars Technica, TechCrunch).
- All feeds are merged, normalized to a common schema, filtered to the last 24 hours, sorted by date, and capped at 10.
- A Code node compiles the articles into a digest, which is passed to an OpenAI-powered AI agent.
- The agent picks the most newsworthy story (or combines 2–3 related ones), fetches full article text via a custom tool, and writes an original 600–1000 word article.
- The AI output is parsed into a title and HTML body, then posted as a draft to WordPress.

## How to set up

1. Add your OpenAI API key under Credentials → OpenAI. The workflow uses `gpt-5.1` by default — swap to any chat model in the "OpenAI Chat Model" node.
2. Add your WordPress API credentials (site URL, username, and application password).
3. Activate the workflow — it runs daily at 8 AM.

## Requirements

- OpenAI account and API key
- WordPress site with application password enabled

## How to customize

- Swap or add RSS feed URLs by duplicating an RSS node and connecting it to the Merge node (increase `numberInputs` accordingly).
- Change the Limit node value to control how many articles the AI reviews.
- Switch the WordPress post status from `draft` to `publish` for immediate publishing.

workflow.name: Write daily tech news with AI using RSS and WordPress

parameters.text:
=You are a professional tech news journalist. Every day you receive a digest of the latest news articles from major tech publications. Your job is to pick the most newsworthy story (or combine 2-3 related stories into one narrative) and write an original, polished news article. ## Today's News Digest {{ $json.articlesSummary }} ## Tools Available - **Fetch Article**: Pass a full article URL (including https://) to read the complete text. Use this to get the full details of the articles you want to write about. ## Your Task 1. Review the headlines and snippets above. 2. Pick the single most important/interesting story, OR pick 2-3 related stories you can weave into one narrative. 3. Use the Fetch Article tool to read the full text of your chosen 2-3 articles. Do NOT fetch more than 3. 4. If a fetch fails (paywall, error), skip it and work with what you have. 5. Write an original news article based on what you've read. ## NEWS ARTICLE STRUCTURE ### Headline Clear, factual, attention-grabbing. ### Lead Paragraph (1 paragraph) The most important facts: who, what, when, where, why. ### Body (3-5 paragraphs, use

subheadings where appropriate) - Key details and context - Background and significance - Multiple perspectives where available - Concrete data, numbers, or specifics ### What's Next (1-2 paragraphs) Implications, what to watch for, what happens next. ## WRITING RULES - Neutral, professional journalistic tone - 600-1000 words - All content ORIGINAL — synthesize and rewrite, never copy - Attribute sources naturally (e.g. "according to Ars Technica", "The Verge reports") - Use

,

, , for formatting - Do NOT include , , or tags ## OUTPUT FORMAT Return in this EXACT format: TITLE: [Your headline] ---CONTENT--- [Full article in HTML]

parameters.name: my_tool

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### Automatically write and publish an original tech news article to WordPress every morning — completely hands-free.

Great for tech bloggers, content creators, or anyone who wants a daily news column without the daily effort.

### How it works
* A schedule trigger fires daily at 8 AM and pulls articles from four RSS feeds (BBC Tech, The Verge, Ars Technica, TechCrunch).
* Articles are merged, normalized, filtered to the last 24 hours, sorted, and capped at 10.
* An OpenAI-powered AI agent picks the most newsworthy story, fetches full article text, and writes an original 600–1000 word article.
* The output is parsed into a title and HTML body, then posted as a draft to WordPress.

### How to use
* Add your OpenAI API key under Credentials.
* Add your WordPress API credentials (site URL + application password).
* Optionally swap RSS feeds, adjust the article limit, or change the post status.
* Activate the workflow — it runs daily at 8 AM.

### Requirements
* OpenAI account and API key
* WordPress site with application password enabled

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/XPKeKXeB7d) or ask in the [Forum](https://community.n8n.io/)!

Happy Hacking!

## 1. Fetch RSS feeds
Pulls the latest articles from BBC Tech, The Verge, Ars Technica, and TechCrunch in parallel, triggered daily at 8 AM.

## 2. Normalize and filter
Standardizes fields across feeds, drops articles older than 24 hours, sorts by date, and caps the list at 10 items.

## 3. AI writes the article
Compiles headlines into a digest, then an OpenAI agent picks the top story, fetches full text, and writes an original news article.

## 4. Publish to WordPress
Extracts the headline and HTML body from the AI output and creates a WordPress draft post.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
